class.comment:
# Enum-to-String Mapping: FluentValidation Regression Test
## Test Coverage
This folder contains positive-only regression tests for enum-to-string mapping scenarios in FluentValidation.
### Test Cases
#### 1. CQRS: CreateOrderCommand
**Pattern**: Command DTO with Create Entity Action
**DTO Fields**: - `Status: OrderStatus` (enum type) - Mapped to Order entity - Expected: `.IsInEnum()` validator - `Notes: string` (string type) - Explicit Validations stereotype: MaxLength = 100 - Expected: `.MaximumLength(100)` validator
**Validator File**: `CreateOrderCommandValidator.cs`
#### 2. Traditional Service: OrderService
**Pattern**: Service with Operation Parameters (no DTO)
**Operation Parameters**: - `status: OrderStatus` (enum parameter) - Validates using domain enum values - Expected: `.IsInEnum()` validator (via FluentValidation service validators) - `notes: string` (string parameter) - Inherits constraints from mapped entity attribute (Order.Notes) - Expected: `.MaximumLength(100)` validator
**Service File**: `OrderService.cs` **Operation**: `ProcessOrder(status: OrderStatus, notes: string): void`
## Domain Model Constraints
**Entity**: Order
Each attribute demonstrates BOTH constraint approaches:
| Attribute | Domain Constraints | RDBMS Constraints | Purpose | |-----------|-------------------|------------------|----------| | StatusText | Text Limits (50) | Text Constraints (50) | Show dual stereotypes | | Notes | Text Limits (100) | Text Constraints (100) | Show dual stereotypes |
**Why Both?** - Domain Constraints: Validation metadata (used by FluentValidation modules) - Text Constraints: Database schema metadata (used by EF Core / RDBMS modules) - Both can coexist and serve different purposes
## Positive Assertions Only
Both test cases use **presence-based assertions**:
### CreateOrderCommand Assertions
1. ✅ `Status` field generates `.IsInEnum()` rule 2. ✅ `Notes` field generates `.MaximumLength(100)` rule 3. ✅ Code compiles without errors
### OrderService Assertions
1. ✅ Service generates with `ProcessOrder` operation 2. ✅ Parameters properly typed (enum and string) 3. ✅ Code compiles without errors
## Test Stability
✅ **No Negative Snapshots**: Uses positive assertions only ✅ **CI/CD Stable**: Won't fail on unrelated changes ✅ **Guards Behavior**: Verifies what should be present ✅ **Comprehensive Coverage**: CQRS + Traditional Services patterns
## Generated Files
### Domain - `Order.cs` - Entity with dual constraint stereotypes - `OrderStatus.cs` - Enum (Pending, Approved, Rejected)
### Application (CQRS) - `CreateOrderCommand.cs` - Command DTO - `CreateOrderCommandValidator.cs` - Validator with rules - `CreateOrderCommandHandler.cs` - Handler
### Application (Services) - `OrderService.cs` - Service implementation - `IOrderService.cs` - Service interface
## Future Enhancement
Once negative snapshot support becomes available: - Verify absence of unintended rules - Validate complete validator chains - Assert rule ordering and composition
class.name: EnumToStringMapping
